items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** f36c2590fdd199291297b197fc5b56a1040cb2e7 (PR #57773) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed Introduced a new `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` package (1.0.0-beta.1) using the new provisioning generator. The package adds declarative provisioning support for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, including these resource types: - `MySqlFlexibleServer` - `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` - `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` - `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` - `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` - `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2` - `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` - `MySqlFlexibleServersCapability` - `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` - `AdvancedThreatProtection` ## Gaps Found - **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** ã no guidance on how to authenticate when using the provisioning library - **`Examples` section is empty** ã no code snippets showing how to create a MySQL Flexible Server, add a firewall rule, configure a database, etc. - **`Next steps` section is empty** ã no links to related docs, samples, or the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) workflow - **No sample files exist** ã `tests/Samples/` directory does not exist; there are no test-backed snippets for snippet extraction
Þ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`** Replace the empty sections with content as follows: 1. Fill `### Authenticate the Client` with a note that no explicit authentication is needed ã the provisioning library generates Bicep/ARM templates; authentication occurs when deploying via `azd` or the Azure CLI. 2. Fill `## Examples` with at least two snippet placeholders: one for creating a basic MySQL Flexible Server and one for adding a firewall rule. 3. Fill `## Next steps` with links to: - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) getting started - Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation Example README diff for the `## Examples` section: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer Infrastructure infra = new(); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new("mysqlServer", MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { Location = AzureLocation.EastUS, Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D4ds_v4", Tier = ServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose, }, }; infra.Add(server); ``` ### Add a Firewall Rule ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new("allowAll") { StartIpAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIpAddress = "255.255.255.255", Parent = server, }; infra.Add(firewallRule); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Create the sample test file** at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlProvisioning.cs` Use `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` samples as the canonical example for test file structure. The file should: - Wrap examples in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` - Use `[Test]` (NUnit) attribute on each method - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` Suggested snippets: - `MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer` ã demonstrates creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer` - `MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule` ã demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` as a child resource - `MySqlProvisioningAddDatabase` ã demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` Reference an existing provisioning sample, e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/tests/Samples/`, for the test class shape. 2. **Add snippet placeholders in `README.md`** under `## Examples` in the exact format: ```` ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` must contain these sections. Currently missing/incomplete sections: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started ã Install the package | ã Present | | Getting started ã Prerequisites | ã Present | | Getting started ã Authenticate the Client | ã Empty | | Key concepts | ã Present (brief) | | Examples | ã Empty | | Troubleshooting | ã Present | | Next steps | ã Empty | | Contributing | ã Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. For the **Authenticate the Client** section, adapt the pattern from other provisioning packages (e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md`): note that this library generates infrastructure-as-code (Bicep) and authentication is handled at deployment time via `azd` or ARM. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` ã injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders; verify the README diff looks correct 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` ã regenerates public API listing files (only needed if public types changed) 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [MySql Provisioning] Docs: Add examples and complete README for Azure.Provisioning.MySql
